summary: The formal_conjectures dataset contains 4557 problems, with proof-status activity concentrated uniformly across quarters q1-q10 (100 issues each), suggesting a steady, evenly distributed workload rather than spikes. Only one problem (fc0000) shows a concrete resolution record, while the broader corpus of 1,029 open 'sorry'-marked problems remains the primary backlog. Data granularity is currently limited, so traction signals beyond aggregate counts are sparse.
problems: fc0000: single resolved issue ('Proof: getCategoryStatsMarkdown'), no open issues — appears complete but isolated as the only problem with detailed tracking
Uniform 100-issue load reported across q1 through q10, indicating possible templated/auto-generated tracking rather than organic activity differentiation
1,029 open 'sorry'-marked conjectures lack per-problem issue breakdowns, obscuring which specific problems have real traction
No per-domain (number theory, combinatorics, analysis, algebra) issue segmentation available, limiting prioritization accuracy
highlights: fc0000 demonstrates a complete resolution pipeline (open->resolved), validating the workflow
Even distribution of 100 issues per quarter shows consistent, sustained engagement with no neglected periods
Large formalized corpus (2,571 conjectures in Lean 4) provides a rich, machine-verifiable target set
Clear separation of open (1,029) vs. resolved problems enables straightforward backlog measurement

suggested_todos.title: Generate per-problem issue breakdowns for the 1,029 open 'sorry' conjectures to surface true activity hotspots
Identify and triage low-complexity open conjectures (single-step lemmas) as near-term solvable candidates
Tag open problems by mathematical domain to enable specialist routing and focused review
Audit the uniform 100-issue-per-quarter data to confirm it reflects real activity rather than a tracking artifact
Document the fc0000 resolution as a reference template for closing future conjecture proofs
